OPPOSITION TO RESPONDENT MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT FILED BY PETITIONER VORTEX MARINE CONSTRUCTION, INC. FETTIG, BLAISE

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. RESPONDENT COWHEY PACIFIC DRILLING, INC.'S MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT IS CONTINUED TO MAY 21, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302, ON THE COURT'S MOTION. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: SUSAN CROZIER, CSR # 4761
MINI MINUTES FOR MAY-13-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. RESPONDENT COWHEY PACIFIC DRILLING, INC'S MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE COURT DENIES DISCRETIONARY RELIEF. UNDER ETERNITY INVESTMENTS, INC. V. BROWN (2007) 151 CAL.APP.4TH 739, RESPONDENT CAN RELY ON CCP 473 FOR RELIEF FROM THE PETITION TO VACATE DEADLINE. NEVERTHELESS, RESPONDENT FAILED TO FILE THIS MOTION WITHIN A "REASONABLE TIME" AS REQUIRED BY CCP 473. PARAGRAPHS 21- 22 OF THE LOSCH DECLARATION SHOW THAT RESPONDENT KNEW OF THE ARBITRATOR'S RELATIONSHIP WITH PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL IN OCTOBER OF 2012. PARAGRAPH 24 DISCLOSES THAT RESPONDENT KNEW ABOUT THE ARBITRATOR'S HEALTH CONDITION IN DECEMBER OF 2012. RESPONDENT DELAYED FILED THE INSTANT MOTION UNTIL APRIL 2, 2013. IN ADDITION, THE UNDERLYING GROUNDS FOR RELIEF ARE TENUOUS. THERE IS LITTLE INFORMATION REGARDING THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN THE ARBITRATOR AND PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL AND IT APPEARS THAT THE RELATIONSHIP IS TOO ATTENUATED TO WARRANT DISCLOSURE. SEE NEMECK & COLE V. HORN, (2012) 208 CAL.APP.4TH 641, 646-647. RESPONDENT ALSO FAILS TO SHOW THAT DISCLOSURE OF THE MEDICAL CONDITION WAS NECESSARY. IT IS UNCLEAR WHETHER THE ARBITRATOR EVEN KNEW OF HIS CONDITION DURING THE ARBITRATION. THE REQUEST FOR MANDATORY RELIEF IS DENIED BECAUSE THIS CASE DOES NOT INVOLVE A DEFAULT, DEFAULT JUDGMENT OR DISMISSAL. SEE ENGLISH V. IKON BUSINESS SOLUTIONS, INC., (2001) 94 CAL. APP. 4TH 130, 138. THE REQUEST FOR EQUITABLE RELIEF IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD KRAMER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

Mtn To Vacate Judgmnt
Off Calendar May-21-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ronald K. Losch, Esq. of Losch & Ehrlich, Tel. 415.956.8400, for respondent and moving party;
Howard G. Curtis, Esq. of McInerney & Dillon, Tel. 510.465.7100, for petitioner and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. RESPONDENT COWHEY PACIFIC DRILLING, INC'S Motion To Vacate Judgment is denied. The Court denies discretionary relief. Under Eternity Investments, Inc. v. Brown (2007) 151 Cal.App.4th 739, respondent can rely on CCP 473 for relief from the petition to vacate deadline. Nevertheless, respondent failed to file this motion within a "reasonable time" as required by CCP 473. Paragraphs 21- 22 of the Losch declaration show that respondent knew of the arbitrator's relationship with plaintiff's counsel in October of 2012. Paragraph 24 discloses that respondent knew about the arbitrator's health condition in December of 2012. Respondent delayed filed the instant motion until April 2, 2013. In addition, the underlying grounds for relief are tenuous. There is little information regarding the relationship between the arbitrator and plaintiff's counsel and it appears that the relationship is too attenuated to warrant disclosure. See Nemeck & Cole v. Horn, (2012) 208 Cal.App.4th 641, 646-647. Respondent also fails to show that disclosure of the medical condition was necessary. It is unclear whether the arbitrator even knew of his condition during the arbitration. The request for mandatory relief is denied because this case does not involve a default, default judgment or dismissal. See English v. IKON Business Solutions, Inc., (2001) 94 Cal. App. 4th 130, 138. The request for equitable relief is denied. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Richard Kramer; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
